# **ADR RHAISTRAT-1109 “Integrate eval-hub Evaluation Scores with OCI for Dynamic Model Cards”**

| Date | January , 2026 |
| :---- | :---- |
| Scope | EvalHub (TrustyAI) |
| Status | Draft |
| Authors | TrustyAI |
| Supersedes | N/A |
| Superseded by: | N/A |
| Tickets | [RHAISTRAT-979](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHAISTRAT-979), [RHAISTRAT-1147](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHAISTRAT-1147) |
| Other docs: |  |

## **What**

The \[EvalHub\] user can enable and use an option to record results from an evaluation run, to OCI artifact

## **Why**

- *As the RHAI platform strategy for evaluations ([RHAISTRAT-26](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHAISTRAT-26)) is that any new evaluation capability should be framework-agnostic, we should provide \[earlier\] [RHAISTRAT-513](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHAISTRAT-513) capabilities that are decoupled from TrustyAI.*  
- *This \[RFE\] is for extending eval-hub with similar capabilities so it works across evaluation and benchmark frameworks registered in eval-hub, even the BYO eval/benchmark frameworks.*  
- Provide an option to persist evaluation run results for longer term durability and auditing, surviving PVC, S3, etc as an OCI Artifact  
- Provide a foundation for future extending this capability for Signature, Attestation of the OCI Artifact

## **Goals**

* If the EvalHub User chooses to exercise this option, the Evaluation Run results (files of eval-results, eval-traces[^1], eval-logs, etc) will be persisted as an OCI Artifact  
* the OCI Artifact shall also include the “common EvalHub json” format (per PM request)  
* This capability is optionally invoked by the Eval-Hub (on eval task submission) and therefore the overall eval-hub will have a provision to enact this  
  * by also adding the necessary reference to the RHOAI OCI Connection

## **Non-Goals**

* Mutate existing KServe ModelCar, mutate existing OCI Artifact/Container/Images  
* OTEL links (not in scope of RFE): this RFE is meant to preserve the full artifacts produced by the evaluation frameworks  
  * However, if any OTEL metric or OTEL-trace is produced as part of this RFE, it shall be consistent with the produced OCI Artifact, i.e.: the content of the OCI Artifact must be consistent with the evaluation results files, and in turn consistent with the OTEL metric/traces  
* Revisit the RHOAI concept of Connection, tenancy, etc;  
  * However, implementation shouldn’t be strictly reliant to the way RHOAI Connection conforms today, and be compatible with future changes with minimal efforts.

## **How**

Strongly recommend to use OCI Artifact as **Image** mediaType (ie. “FROM scratch”) with artifactType (e.g. “x-application/evalhub”) to make this available for ImageVolume mounting support of OCP 4.20 (which does not support arbitrary OCI Artifact).

![][image1]

1. Extending the eval-hub-sdk so that (once called, "instructed" by eval-hub) the Results and the Traces and any other logs can be persisted in an OCI Artifact  
2. The OCI Artifact may (or may not) have OCI Subject/Referrer to the ModelCar (same repo case)  
   1. when may-not, we could annotate the reference  
3. Since eval-hub is always the "driver", while high-level Results metadata are being stored in metadata registries, we can have a link from those metadata registries (MLflow, KF registry, ...) to the OCI Artifact of the eval results and traces

To expand on point 2, same-repo case allows to use the OCI Referrer(Subject) API spec:  
![][image2]  
In this case:

- the OCI Artifact (e.g. acmeorg/evalresult:v1) manifest having Subject the KServer ModelCar (e.g. acmeorg/model:modelcar) allows the OCI Artifact to “point” and refer to the ModelCar  
- the OCI registry can then provide by means of infrastructure all the OCI Artifact(s) which are being referred by the ModelCar 

To expand on point 2, not-same-repo case, only soft references can be used in the OCI Artifact, and rely more on the metadata-registries (MLflow, KF registry, …) for the linkage (as demonstrated in the video for [one example](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WqeSIbyLP8g&t=645s)):  
![][image3]

In this case:

- the OCI Artifact (e.g. acmeorg/evalresult:v1) cannot specify Subject and so for the OCI Artifact to “point” and refer to the ModelCar, an OCI Annotation will be used  
- the OCI registry cannot automatically provide from the ModelCar any referred artifact  
- the metadata-registries (MLflow, KF registry, …) can be leveraged for the linkage as demonstrated in the video for [one example](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WqeSIbyLP8g&t=645s)

The production of OCI Artifact can be done efficiently by re-using earlier “specialization” [RHAISTRAT-513](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHAISTRAT-513) in LMEval to produce the OCI Artifact, but now generalized for the eval-hub-sdk, and can be achieved with minimal efforts.

The [eval-hub-sdk interface](https://github.com/eval-hub/eval-hub-sdk/blob/8642c90989b83aa7ced12dc1503542f4e7835809/src/evalhub/adapter/models/framework.py#L106) will be expanded with a function dedicated to point to the files which need to be persisted,  for example:

```py
@abstractmethod
def job_files(self, job_id: str) -> EvaluationJobFilesLocation:
    """Files location of an evaluation job.

    Args:
        job_id: The job identifier

    Returns:
        EvaluationJobFiles: the location of the files
    """
    pass

```

In this way, the user will provide only the location of the files, without knowing the internals of OCI production.

The eval-hub-sdk will generalize [RHAISTRAT-513](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHAISTRAT-513) to produce the OCI Artifact, for example:

```py
async def persist_job_files_oci(self, job_id: str, oci_ref: str) -> PersistResponse | None:
    """Persist all files of an evaluation job as OCI Artifact in the specified repository.

    Args:
        job_id: The job identifier
    oci_ref: The location where to persist (Push) the OCI Artifact

    Returns:
        bool: The 
    """

    # ...
    what_to_persist = self.job_files(job_id)
    # ...
```

In this way, the end-user doesn’t need to be concerned about how the OCI Artifact needs to be produced, but only *what* needs to be persisted (declarative approach by User, and enacted by the \-sdk).

The credential for the OCI repository will use standard RHOAI OCI Connection mechanism, so that will work both in K8s and non-K8s environments.  
In pragmatic terms:

- as usual RHOAI Connection, OCI Connection will be mounted in standard K8s way to the workload, so that the function have access to the specific credentials  
- when running in non-K8s environment, standard docker/oci auth.json can be leveraged by the function as already demonstrated

To map on the Eval-Hub overall, specific provision will be linked so that when the User submits the Eval task on Eval-Hub, the User can specify to opt-in to this capability, by also specifying the required RHOAI OCI Connection. 

Functional requirement is met by means of existing functionality and compatible with existing RHOAI concepts and principles (e.g.: OCI Connection resource in RHOAI).

The functionality when running on Kubernetes leverages the EvalHub sidecar capabilities so that the Adapter sdk code doesn’t require direct access to the OCI connection secret, but the bearer challenge is performed by the EvalHub sidecar acting as a proxy, aligning with the current sidecar design.

[^1]:  not to be confused with OTEL traces; by eval-traces this document refers to additional supporting files, dumps, logs in addition to the typical “results file” or report. Typically this eval-traces file(s) contains prompts and answers and any additional operation results in the specific format of the evaluation, supporting the computation of the overall results.
